user_query: I’m preparing to submit my dissertation and want the small static site I’ll include in my submission package to be indexable and easy to find. Please audit and optimize the HTML pages in input/site/ for basic on-page SEO using the keyword list in input/keywords.csv and the metadata in input/metadata.yaml. Produce updated HTML, a sitemap and robots file, and a concise audit report I can attach to my submission notes. Requirements: 1) Do not modify files in input/. Write all outputs to output/. 2) Inspect input/site/ to discover all .html files. 3) For each HTML page, write an optimized version to output/site/ with the same filename. Ensure: a) exactly one with a concise, unique title (recommended ≤ 60 characters); b) a meta description (recommended 50–160 characters) that summarizes the page and includes at least one keyword from input/keywords.csv that already appears on that page; c) one visible <h1>; d) a rel="canonical" tag built as {base_url}/{filename} using base_url from input/metadata.yaml; e) a meta robots tag index,follow; f) basic Open Graph tags (og:title, og:description, og:type where index.html may be website and content pages article); g) for dissertation.html only, insert JSON-LD structured data of type Thesis using fields from input/metadata.yaml (at minimum: name, author name, author identifier (ORCID), inLanguage, datePublished (year), identifier (DOI if present), and publisher (university)); h) add alt text to any <img> missing alt (use a short descriptive alt based on the filename and nearby context). 4) Generate output/sitemap.xml listing all discovered pages with <loc> using base_url from input/metadata.yaml and the page filename (omit lastmod if not determinable). 5) Generate output/robots.txt that allows all crawling and points to the sitemap (lines: User-agent: *; Allow: /; Sitemap: {base_url}/sitemap.xml). 6) Compute and include in your audit: a) inventory of processed files; b) per-page metrics: word_count, title_length, description_length, h1_count, images_without_alt_before, images_without_alt_after; c) keyword coverage: for each keyword in input/keywords.csv, report occurrences in dissertation.html and across the whole site; and d) coverage summary: keywords_total, keywords_covered (≥1 occurrence site-wide), and coverage_ratio = keywords_covered/keywords_total. 7) Write a human-readable audit to output/seo_report.md with these sections: Files processed; Per-page metrics; Keyword coverage; Coverage summary; Changes made (bullet list of edits per file). 8) Also write a machine-readable summary to output/seo_summary.json with: total_pages, pages_fixed, total_word_count, keywords_total, keywords_covered, coverage_ratio (decimal), keyword_counts_by_page (object mapping page filename to a mapping of keyword->count), and per_page_metrics (array with one object per page including the metrics in 6b). Use only the local files; do not use the Internet. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
